rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area; prime areas CHF 3,500-4,500; outer suburbs CHF 2,200-2,800; mid estimate ~CHF 2,600 (~$2,900 → discounted for non-prime)

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; Swiss utilities high; internet ~CHF 60

groceries.note: home cooking; Migros/Coop; Geneva food costs very high; $621 reported monthly avg

diningOut.note: dining out extremely expensive; cheap meal ~$22; mid-range dinner for two ~$80-100

transit.note: TPG monthly pass ~CHF 70 (~$80); cross-border commuters pay more

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; Geneva coworking premium-priced; expect CHF 280-400/mo

simData.note: SIM-only plan (Salt/Sunrise/Yallo); Swiss mobile costs elevated; ~CHF 30-40/mo

misc.note: gym (~$80), toiletries, entertainment, mandatory health insurance contribution buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Geneva furnished lets are stable year-round for medium-term stays.

evidence.claim: Geneva solo monthly cost with rent
evidence.quote: estimated monthly costs are $1,804 excluding rent, and $4,121 including rent
rent ranges from CHF 3,500–4,500 per month for 1-bedroom apartments in prime areas like Eaux-Vives, down to CHF 2,200–2,800 in outer suburbs like Meyrin or Lancy
Geneva's average cost of living is $3,731, ranking it in the top 0.2% of the most expensive cities in the world
